nodes.title: Introduction
nodes.summary: Sets the context for applying the standard in UK defence acquisition and through-life support, explaining why it exists and who it applies to. It orients readers to the policy and governance backdrop before the requirements chapters.

nodes.title: Safety Management Requirements for Defence Systems
nodes.summary: Provides the top-level framing for the standard, describing how safety is achieved, assured, and managed for defence Products, Services and/or Systems (PSS). It acts as the structural entry point that organizes and introduces the major requirement areas across the document.

nodes.title: Technical Information
nodes.summary: Defines the document’s technical framing and how external standards and guidance are used, including the distinction between normative and informative references. It is the main navigation point for understanding dependencies and how referenced material affects compliance.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: Establishes the terminology and interpretation rules used throughout the standard, including how mandatory language is to be read. It is essential for consistent understanding and application of requirements across all chapters.

nodes.title: Safety Management System
nodes.summary: Specifies expectations for establishing and operating a contractor Safety Management System (SMS), including planning, agreement, maintenance, and reporting via the Safety Management Plan (SMP). It is the core governance chapter for how safety work is organized and controlled.

nodes.title: General Requirements
nodes.summary: Defines cross-cutting contractual and process requirements such as deviation handling, legal/policy alignment, sub-contracting controls, deliverables, and safety information management. It provides the baseline obligations that apply across the safety lifecycle and other chapters.

nodes.title: Roles and Responsibilities
nodes.summary: Sets requirements for defining, delegating, and maintaining safety roles, including competence expectations and committee participation. It ensures accountability and capability are in place to execute the safety management and engineering activities.

nodes.title: Interfaces
nodes.summary: Requires identification and management of organisational, technical, and external interfaces, including recording assumptions and reconciling information between parties. It is key to controlling safety risk where responsibilities and system boundaries interact.

nodes.title: Safety Audits
nodes.summary: Defines audit and assurance expectations, including reporting, auditor independence, access for independent audit, and handling remedial actions. It provides the mechanism for checking that safety management and evidence remain credible and effective.

nodes.title: Safety Requirements, Hazard and Risk Analysis
nodes.summary: Provides the core technical requirements for hazard identification, hazard tracking, safety requirements definition and management, design for safety, safety analysis, and risk estimation/evaluation. It is central to demonstrating that risks are understood, controlled, and justified with evidence.

nodes.title: Safety Reporting
nodes.summary: Specifies required safety reporting outputs, including the Information Set Safety Summary (ISSS) and the Safety Case and associated reports. It defines how safety evidence and arguments are packaged for review, decision-making, and through-life assurance.

nodes.title: Supply and Change Management
nodes.summary: Defines how build state and change control are managed so that safety is maintained when designs, components, or configurations change. It links change activities to required updates of hazard analysis, safety case evidence, and the information set.

nodes.title: Supporting Systems In-Service
nodes.summary: Covers in-service safety support, including collection and management of safety-related operational data, monitoring and analysis, and remedial action. It ensures operational feedback is used to maintain and update safety justification through life.

nodes.title: Service Provision
nodes.summary: Defines safety requirements for delivering services, including pre-commencement safety case reporting/approval and planning for normal and emergency situations. It addresses how risk to life is managed during service delivery and how MOD is supported in operational risk management.

nodes.title: ASEMS DE&S Acquisition Safety and Environmental Management System
nodes.summary: Key MOD acquisition safety and environmental management framework used as supporting guidance for safety management planning and governance. It is a common external dependency for aligning contractor safety management with DE&S processes.

nodes.title: Knowledge in Defence website
nodes.summary: MOD knowledge portal referenced for accessing defence standards and related competence/supporting resources. It is a recurring dependency for locating authoritative materials referenced by multiple parts of the document.
